english: C., Lex. also čá-ga-pa (cha ga pa), grasshopper.

pf. and imp. soṅ (song) (the regular form čhas (chas) being nearly obs. at present), in W. the usual word for འགྲོ་བ་ ('gro ba) to go, in B. little used and only in later writings, 1. to go, *sór-te čá-če* (sor te cha che) to retire, to retreat slowly; *da ča yiṅ* (da cha ying) or *da čeṅ* (da cheng) adieu, good bye, farewell! *da čeṅ žu* (da cheng zhu) resp., your servant! (in taking leave); *'á-ru-soṅ* ('a ru song) go thither, or that way! *'á-ru ma ča* ('a ru ma cha) do not go to this place, do not step this way! to travel, *gyál-la* (gyal la) (or *dé-mo, yág-po*) (de mo, yag po) *ča žig* (cha zhig) I wish you a safe journey, a pleasant trip to you! *lóg-te čá-če* (log te cha che) to return, to go or come back; *tiṅ-la čá-če* (ting la cha che) to follow, to come after or later; *ča čug* (cha chug) let (him) go! give (it) up! let (it) alone! to be gone, consumed, spent, used, wasted, *šiṅ máṅ-po ča yiṅ* (shing mang po cha ying) a great deal of wood will go, will be consumed. -- 2. to become, grow, get, turn, *tsan ča dug* (tsan cha dug) it grows night, it is getting dark; *gas čá čè* (gas cha che) to grow old: *nág-po soṅ* (nag po song) that has turned black; *šès-kan čá-čè* (shes kan cha che) to get information; also with la: *bág-ma-la čá-ba* (bag ma la cha ba) (= bág-mar gró-ba, gyúr-ba (bag mar gro ba, gyur ba)) to become a bride Ma.; *mán-lami ča* (man la mi cha) this is not used for medicine. -- 3. with a supine (B.) or a verbal root (col.): to be about, to be on the point, to be going, sléb-tu čá-bai tse (sleb tu cha bai tse) when they were on the point of arriving Mil.; nyí-ma འཆར་དུ་ཆ་བ་དང་ ('char du cha ba dang) when the sun was just going to rise Mil.; *me ši ča dug* (me shi cha dug) the fire is on the point of going out; *nad ži ča dug* (nad zhi cha dug), the disease is decreasing. -- 4. with the gerund it expresses a continuous progress, a gradual operation, an effect by little and little, *ču pél-te ča dug* (chu pel te cha dug) the water increases from day to day. -- 5. with the inf. it is used in the sense of the future tense, or like the Greek μέλλειν: to intend, to purpose, *ci šríd-de dir šriṅ-če ča dug* (ci shrid de dir shring che cha dug) how long does he (do you etc.) intend to stay? *nam lug sád-če ča dug* (nam lug sad che cha dug) when are you going to kill the sheep?
a kind of little ornament worn in the ears Ld.

1. thing, implement, instrument, e.g. a musical instrument Dzl., a surgical instr. Med. -- 2. clothing, dress, mi-sdúg-pai ča-byad-čan (mi sdug pai cha byad can) poorly clothed, ragged Mil.; external appearance, also of animals.

1. oak, also mon-čá-ra (mon cha ra) (on account of its growing only on the southern ranges of the Himalaya mountains, inhabited mostly by Non-Tibetans) in several species, with pointed, evergreen leaves, a tree much inferior in beauty to the English oak. čá-ra preu (cha ra preu) Sch.: 'the stunted or dwarf-oak'. -- 2. also ča-ri, ča-li, ča-lú (cha ri, cha li, cha lu), a coarse sort of blanket made of yak's hair.

1. C. implements, instruments, required for the carrying on of a business. -- 2. W. things, effects, luggage. -- 3. Tar. 43, 18: čá-lag daṅ bčas-pa rdzógs-par šès-pa (cha lag dang bcas pa rdzogs par shes pa) Schf.: 'the systematic and complete understanding'.
joined with rdéb-pa (rdeb pa) Lex. and Mil., meaning not known; Wts. gives: petite lance des bonzes.
= há-lam (ha lam), some; for the most part, rather C.
part, portion, share, lús-kyi ča-šàs (lus kyi cha shas) a part of the body, a limb etc.
